3.happening: Start the best sum at the first element.
3.why: Every non-empty array has this one-element subarray, so it's a valid starting answer.
3.invariant: max_sum is the best subarray sum found so far.

4.happening: Seed the best run ending here with the first element too.
4.why: current_sum tracks the best subarray that ends at the current position.
4.invariant: current_sum is the max sum of a subarray ending at i.

5.why: Kadane folds in one element at a time.
5.invariant: Elements before i are already accounted for in current_sum and max_sum.

6.happening: The previous run ({current_sum}) is a drag — restart the subarray at {nums[i]}.
6.why: When the running sum is negative, dropping it and starting fresh can only help.
6.invariant: current_sum is now the best subarray ending at i.

6.why: The running sum is still positive help, so keep the subarray going.
6.invariant: current_sum is now the best subarray ending at i.

7.happening: Is this run ({current_sum}) the best we've seen? Update max_sum if so.
7.why: The answer is the best run ending at any position.
7.invariant: max_sum is the largest current_sum over all positions so far.

8.happening: Return the largest subarray sum found ({max_sum}).
8.why: One pass suffices — each element folds in in O(1).
8.invariant: max_sum is the global maximum subarray sum.

init.happening: Seeding both sums with the first element.
init.why: We need a valid starting answer before scanning.
init.invariant: max_sum and current_sum both equal nums[0].

loop.happening: Scanning the array one element at a time.
loop.why: Kadane extends the answer element by element.
loop.invariant: Everything left of the cursor is folded in.

update.happening: Deciding to extend the current run or restart it here.
update.why: Keeping a negative running sum would only hurt; restarting cuts the loss.
update.invariant: current_sum is the best subarray ending at i.

check.happening: Keeping the best run seen so far.
check.why: The answer is the maximum run over all end positions.
check.invariant: max_sum is the best subarray sum up to i.

return.why: Achieved in a single O(n) pass with O(1) memory.
return.invariant: max_sum is the global maximum subarray sum.
